channel.title: The Fix by JB - Jonathan Barlow
channel.description: Insights on leadership, systems thinking, technology, and Detroit from Jonathan Barlow.

item.title: The Workforce System Isn't Failing — It's Misaligned
item.description: $3.6 billion spent on workforce development annually. 87% of employers still can't find talent. The system isn't failing — it was never aligned.

item.title: We Fund Startups. We Don't Build Businesses.
item.description: 5.5 million new business applications filed last year. Half will close within five years. We celebrate the launch but nobody builds the boring middle.

item.title: America Isn't Broke — It's Disconnected
item.description: GDP is up. Markets are up. Employment is technically strong. But 60% of Americans still live paycheck to paycheck. The problem isn't money — it's architecture.

item.title: Why Your Network Isn't Working
item.description: You collect connections but don't build systems around them. Your network fails because you treat it like a Rolodex instead of infrastructure.

item.title: Build for the Person Who Stays
item.description: Most urban projects build for the "ideal user" who doesn't exist. Real impact means building for the person who stayed through every crisis.

item.title: Why Every Crisis is an Innovation Opportunity
item.description: Three years ago, Detroit's water infrastructure failed. While others saw catastrophe, I saw a laboratory for building resilient urban systems.

item.title: Programs vs. Systems: Why Detroit Keeps Starting Over
item.description: Detroit doesn't have a program problem. It has a systems problem. Every year, new initiatives launch with press releases and ribbon cuttings, yet the same problems persist.

item.title: Most Leadership Advice Is Backwards
item.description: They tell you to think big and start small. I say think in systems and start with structure.
